exemplar.title: What are the implications of having, or not having, knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: What are the implications of having, or not having, knowledge?

strengths: Clearly identifies three distinct real-world objects with specific contexts (EpiPen, Trinity-test bomb, Apple Watch)
Consistent links between each object and the chosen IA prompt, showing awareness of ethical and knowledge implications
Most points are supported by factual references (historical dates, statistics, citations), demonstrating an attempt at evidenceâbased justification
weaknesses: TOK analysis often remains broad and descriptive rather than probing key concepts (e.g., power, responsibility) with depth
Several assertions (e.g., âtechnology controls practically everythingâ) are unsupported or lack precise real-world anchoring
Discussion lacks explicit knowledge questions, counter-arguments, and deeper engagement with ways of knowing, leading to uneven precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the middle band (5â6) descriptors: three objects are clearly identified with real-world contexts and linked to prompt 7, and many points are supported by evidence. However, the analysis is uneven in depth and clarity, relying on common-sense ethical claims rather than sustained TOK exploration. Key weaknesses include unsupported broad assertions, incomplete argumentation, and a lack of explicit knowledge questions or counter-arguments. On balance, the work justifies a mid-band mark of 5 out of 10, reflecting solid identification and some justification, but insufficient precision and depth for the higher bands.

annotations.comment: Wellâplaced reference to user responsibility demonstrates grasp of the ethical dimension of knowledge use.
Pose a knowledge question regarding how different ways of knowing might alter interpretation of biometric data from the watch.
Clear and concise statement of the selected IA prompt and theme establishes strong context for the exhibition.
Opening claim that âtechnology controls practically everythingâ is broad and unsupported; consider anchoring this assertion with a specific example or data.
Introduce clear definitions of âknowledgeâ and âimplicationsâ in the introduction to focus the analysis and guide the reader.
Caption provides identification but misses an explicit connection to TOK analysis; consider adding a brief note linking this object to your knowledge question.
Effective historical reference to Kaplanâs interpretation demonstrates engagement with the way of knowing of interpretation.
Discussion of positive implications remains descriptive and lacks a clear knowledge question or counterclaim to drive TOK analysis forward.
Consider integrating discussion of specific ways of knowing (e.g., emotion, reason) when explaining how knowledge of anaphylaxis spread through communities.
Claims about the inventorâs values improving society are asserted without linking to specific knowledge frameworks or ethical theories.
Reliance on personal anecdote about your sister is engaging but should be balanced with broader statistical evidence to avoid overgeneralization.
Selection of the Trinity test photo is powerful, showing how historical artefacts raise ethical questions about knowledge and power.
Description of ânegative implications of having too much knowledgeâ remains vague; refine by posing a specific knowledge question.
Unpack the concept of scientific responsibility by linking it to specific ways of knowing and ethical frameworks.
Last sentence is cut off, indicating an incomplete thought; ensure full articulation of your argument or justification.
Consider discussing cognitive bias in the decisionâmaking process of the Trinity scientists to deepen analysis of power and ethics.
Opening fragment on page 4 lacks context and should be integrated into a coherent introductory sentence.
Second paragraph on page 4 reiterates earlier claims about limitations without adding depth; tighten focus to avoid repetition.
Introduce a counterâargument about nuclear safeguards or regulation to complicate the claim of scientistsâ unchecked power.
The personal Apple Watch example effectively links technology to trust and responsibility in knowledge acquisition.
Section on misinterpretation ends abruptly midâparagraph; complete the discussion to maintain clarity and flow.
Assertion that âknowledge is better off unknownâ is provocative but lacks realâworld examples or evidence.
Introduce an explicit knowledge question such as âTo what extent does ignorance preserve wellâbeing?â to sharpen analysis.
Concluding paragraph rephrases earlier points without synthesizing insights from all three objects; strengthen by comparing contributions.
Line about trusting the watch highlights engagement with trust as a way of knowing.
Bibliography would benefit from inclusion of TOK theory sources or philosophical works on knowledge.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (access dates, punctuation); apply a uniform referencing style.
Each objectâs section would be clearer if introduced by an explicit knowledge question to guide your analysis.
Final conclusion should explicitly reference IA prompt 7 to remind the reader of the central focus.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:
The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of

than of specific

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
